title: Slow-cooker chickpea curry
subtitle: Tinned chickpeas braised in a spiced tomato sauce
excerpt: Tinned chickpeas slow-cooked with tinned tomatoes, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and garam masala for six hours. The chickpeas absorb the sauce and the sauce thickens around them. A straightforward vegan slow-cooker dinner that keeps well for several days.
sourceNotes: Chana masala (spiced chickpeas) is one of the most widely cooked vegetarian dishes across South Asia and has become a standard in British vegetarian cooking. The slow cooker version appeared in British home kitchens as convenient plant-based cooking grew. The chickpeas absorb the sauce gradually over the long cook in a way that quick-cooked versions do not achieve.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well for up to 3 months.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe and freeze in portions.

content.text: Bloom the spices in oil before adding them. Cumin and coriander dropped directly into the slow cooker produce a raw, dusty flavour rather than a warm, rounded one. One minute in hot oil on the hob before everything else goes in makes a measurable difference to the finished curry.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a pan over medium heat. Fry the {{onion}} for 5 minutes. Add the {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{coriander-ground}}, {{turmeric}}, {{chilli-powder}}, and {{tomato-puree}} and cook for 1 minute. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{chickpeas-tinned}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Cook on low for 6 hours. Stir in the {{garam-masala}} and {{spinach}} (if using) at the end — the spinach wilts in 2 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning.

items.symptom: Chickpeas very soft and beginning to collapse
items.cause: Six hours is long for tinned chickpeas
items.fix: Reduce to 5 hours for next time. Slightly broken chickpeas thicken the sauce — not a bad result if the texture is otherwise good
Add a pinch of caster sugar and taste. An extra tablespoon of tomato puree also helps round a sharp sauce

items.cause: Spices not bloomed before slow cooking
items.fix: The blooming step on the hob is essential. Without it the spices taste raw rather than warm and rounded

content.text: Chickpea curry is the simplest vegan slow-cooker dinner in this collection. The ingredients are all from the pantry — nothing fresh needed beyond onion, garlic, and ginger. It keeps well in the fridge for three days and improves with each reheating. Serve with basmati rice and flatbread.
